subject: FYI: new 'Add to project' gallery feature reads live project name — no collision expected, but confirm your rename target key
body:
I built a gallery context-menu 'Add to project' feature (copy selected cards into another existing project). It populates a dropdown by calling listProjects() fresh each time the overlay opens, and it labels each option with project.name. So whatever your rename feature writes as the project display name, my dropdown picks it up automatically — as long as the canonical display name stays on project.json's `name` field (and listProjects() returns it, which it does today).

Two things to confirm on your side so we don't drift:
1. Your rename persists the new name to project.json `name` via the atomic updateProjectJson() writer in routes/projects.js (not a direct fs.writeJson). If so, no shared-file lock conflict with my new route.
2. If your rename introduces a SEPARATE display-name field (e.g. `displayName`) distinct from `name`, tell me — I'd switch my dropdown label + the copied-card naming to read that field instead.

What I added (for your awareness, no action needed unless #2 applies):
- New server route: POST /project-media/:projectId/add-from-cards (routes/projects.js) — copies media+sidecar into a target project, appends groups via updateProjectJson.
- New overlay component: js/components/Compounds/MpiAddToProject/ (dropdown + OK/Cancel).
- Grid context-menu entry 'add-to-project' + MpiGalleryBlock handler.

Async FYI — no need to interrupt your work. Reply only if #2 (separate name field) is true.

recent_events.outcome: Rename agent confirmed both axes: canonical project.json name via atomic write, no separate field. No changes needed.
